description: Standing Privilege-Escalation-Capable Role Assignment on a Subscription
rationale: This is a standing (permanent, always-active) role assignment at subscription scope of a role that can assign other roles - Owner, User Access Administrator, RBAC Administrator, or a custom role with Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write. These roles are the privilege-escalation primitive on the Azure control plane: whoever holds one can grant any role (including to themselves or to a new identity they control) on the subscription. User Access Administrator in particular has no data-plane purpose and rarely has a legitimate reason to be held permanently. A standing assignment (as opposed to a time-bound, approval-based PIM activation) means the privilege is available at all times, so a single compromised principal yields immediate, persistent control over access on the subscription. Applies to any principal type - User, Group, Service Principal, or Managed Identity.
remediation:
Apply least privilege and avoid standing high-privilege access:
  1. Remove unnecessary Owner / User Access Administrator assignments, especially any that are not clearly required.
  2. Replace standing assignments with time-bound, approval-based, MFA-gated elevation using Privileged Identity Management (PIM).
  3. Scope access to the narrowest subscription / resource group / resource that is actually needed.
  4. Review the assignments regularly and monitor role-assignment activity.
